zotero.abstractNote: Empirical process theory for scalar-valued functions is a cornerstone of modern statistical learning theory. However, many contemporary machine learning tasks, such as multi-task learning, structured prediction, and kernel methods, involve vector-valued functions. In this paper, we develop an empirical process theory for vector-valued functions by extending the notion of metric entropy to this setting. Specifically, we provide upper bounds on the metric entropy of H\"older and Sobolev spaces of vector-valued functions. Our results are based on a novel covering number analysis that leverages the geometry of the output space. We demonstrate the utility of our results by providing generalization error bounds for vector-valued kernel ridge regression and multi-task learning.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Algorithmic Learning Theory
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 34th International Conference on Algorithmic Learning Theory
